ps4_thresholds_applied: The ClinGen VCEP gene-specific text for SDHB does not provide any modifications to the PS4 criterion. Therefore, the default guidance from the ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines is applied. These guidelines describe a proband counting approach as a moderate level of evidence for rare variants when formal case-control studies are not available or do not reach statistical significance (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 2). Inclusion criteria: The prevalence of the variant in affected individuals is significantly increased compared to the prevalence in controls (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3). For formal case-control studies, this is met if the OR or RR is >5.0 and the confidence interval does not include 1.0 (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 1). For rare variants without statistically significant case-control data, the observation of the variant in multiple unrelated patients with the same phenotype, and its absence in controls, may be used (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 2). Exclusion criteria: The variant is present in control populations at a frequency inconsistent with pathogenic status (Richards et al., 2015, Page 12).

ps4_mode_citation: The ClinGen VCEP gene-specific text for SDHB does not provide any modifications to the PS4 criterion. Therefore, the default guidance from the ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines is applied. These guidelines describe a proband counting approach as a moderate level of evidence for rare variants when formal case-control studies are not available or do not reach statistical significance (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 2).
ps4_thresholds_applied: Inclusion criteria per ACMG/AMP 2015: The prevalence of the variant in affected individuals is significantly increased compared to the prevalence in controls (Table 3). For formal case-control studies, this is met if the OR or RR is >5.0 and the confidence interval does not include 1.0 (Table 3, PS4 Note 1). For rare variants without statistically significant case-control data, the observation of the variant in multiple unrelated patients with the same phenotype, and its absence in controls, may be used (Table 3, PS4 Note 2). Exclusion: Variant present in controls at a frequency inconsistent with pathogenic status (Page 12).

(2) For formal case-control studies, OR or RR > 5.0 with CI not including 1.0. (3) For rare variants without statistically significant case-control data, observation of the variant in multiple unrelated patients with the same phenotype, and its absence in controls, may be used. Exclusion: Variant present in controls at a frequency inconsistent with pathogenic status. Case thresholds: OR or RR > 5.0 for case-control; for proband counting, multiple unrelated patients required (no specific number defined).

Counting (proband_count mode): Because the cohort consists of unrelated probands, we count one independent affected proband with SCN5A p.Gln912Arg toward 'a'. Controls: Although the study mentions use of 300 controls in variant assessment, there is no per-variant control count for SCN5A p.Gln912Arg; therefore, we did not populate c/d. PS4 strength is not assigned here; only counts are reported, consistent with the Cardinal Rule.

counting_justification: Study-type: Primary case report with molecular testing. The paper identifies 'a novel, heterozygous KCNQ4 mutation, c.806_808delCCT, which led to a p.Ser269del' in a proband from a pedigree with autosomal dominant progressive hearing loss (Clinical features; Fig. 1A, 1B; Sequencing analysis). Phenotype mapping: The authors describe nonsyndromic autosomal dominant sensorineural hearing loss (DFNA2), which maps to the allowed phenotype 'nonsyndromic genetic deafness (MONDO:0019497), Autosomal Dominant.' Variant inclusion and PM2_Supporting: The mutation was 'found neither on the Exome Variant Server nor in the control group of 96 unrelated Japanese individuals with normal hearing,' supporting PM2_Supporting for AD conditions per the GUIDE. Therefore, this proband is eligible to be counted toward PS4 per the ClinGen Hearing Loss VCEP PS4 proband counting path. Independence/deduplication: Only one genotyped affected individual (the proband) is reported; although the pedigree shows additional affected relatives, they were not genotyped in this study and thus are not counted. Default assumption of relatedness is applied—one proband per family. Controls: 96 normal-hearing controls were screened and were variant-negative; since PS4 mode is proband_count, controls are not entered into a 2×2. Alias reconciliation: The paper presents consistent DNA-level nomenclature 'c.806_808delCCT' (also written as 'c.806–808delCCT' and in Table 1 as 'c.806_808del3'), and protein 'p.Ser269del.' The abstract contains a likely typographical variant name 'p.Ser260del'; context and the main text/figures establish that these refer to the same in-frame deletion event. The INPUTS list 'c.803_805CCT[1]' as an alternate representation; given consistent protein change (Ser269del) and locus region (S5–PH), we treat these as the same molecular event for counting, but we only used the forms explicitly present in this document for the 'variant_forms' field. Final tally: a=1 unrelated proband with the variant and allowed phenotype; b=c=d not applicable in proband_count mode. Total proband count = 1.

Variant identification: The paper reports ‘c.806_808delCCT’ leading to ‘p.Ser269del’ in KCNQ4 (Results 3.2; Conclusion). Table 1 also lists ‘c.806_808del3 p.Ser269del’. The abstract contains ‘p.Ser260del’, which is inconsistent with the body text and table; this is treated as a typographical variant name for the same event based on consistent cDNA notation and the repeated ‘p.Ser269del’ elsewhere. The INPUTS include ‘c.803_805CCT[1]’, which likely reflects legacy transcript numbering; treated as an alias of the same in-frame trinucleotide deletion. Phenotype mapping: The family displays autosomal dominant progressive sensorineural hearing loss (DFNA2 context), i.e., nonsyndromic genetic deafness, which is within ALLOWED_PHENOTYPES (ClinGen HL VCEP; MONDO:0019497). Inclusion and independence: Only the proband is explicitly genotyped with the variant (Fig.1A shows a family, but no additional genotyped affected relatives are given). Per the default relatedness rule, one proband is counted (a=1). Zygosity: explicitly ‘heterozygous’ in text. PM2_Supporting prerequisite: The authors state the variant was absent from the Exome Variant Server and not detected in 96 unrelated Japanese controls with normal hearing (‘This mutation was found neither on the Exome Variant Server nor in the control group of 96 unrelated Japanese individuals with normal hearing.’), consistent with extremely low/absent population frequency required for PM2_Supporting in AD conditions. Controls: Although 96 controls tested negative, PS4 mode here is proband_count; therefore b/c/d are not populated. No case-control statistics are reported. No additional unrelated probands with this exact variant are presented in this paper. Deduplication/overlap: Only one family and one genotyped proband are described; no risk of duplicate counting within this document.

counting_justification: Study-type gate: Primary case report with sequencing of KCNQ4 in a patient/family (methods and individual-level data provided), so eligible for counting. Variant identification: The paper reports a heterozygous in-frame deletion in KCNQ4 exon 5 described as c.806_808delCCT resulting in p.Ser269del (multiple instances: Fig. 2A; Table 1; text p. 476–478). The abstract contains a likely typographical inconsistency “p.Ser260del”, but the body consistently uses p.Ser269del with c.806_808delCCT; both strings are captured as aliases. The INPUT variant list includes p.Ser269del and a legacy-like cDNA repeat notation “c.803_805CCT[1]”; due to the instruction to use only the provided GUIDE and DOCUMENT, we treat the paper’s c.806_808delCCT and p.Ser269del as the definitive representation and include the others as aliases without cross-mapping beyond noting inconsistency. Phenotype mapping: The proband is described with “autosomal dominant progressive hearing loss,” “nonsyndromic,” and DFNA2/KCNQ4 context with audiometry (Fig. 1), which maps to the allowed phenotype “nonsyndromic genetic deafness (Autosomal Dominant)” per the GUIDE. Counting (proband_count mode): Default deduplication rule applied—one proband per family unless independence is explicit. Only the proband was sequenced; a single family pedigree is shown (Fig. 1A). Therefore, a = 1 unrelated proband with the variant and the allowed phenotype. Zygosity: explicitly heterozygous. PM2_Supporting prerequisite: The authors state the variant was absent from the Exome Variant Server and not observed in 96 normal-hearing controls, supporting PM2_Supporting for an AD condition per the GUIDE; hence the proband is eligible to count toward PS4. Controls: Although 96 normal-hearing controls were screened with zero occurrences of the variant, proband_count mode does not require nor use control tallies; thus b=c=d are left at 0 by design. No other affected individuals with this variant were reported in this paper. No overlap with other families/studies is indicated within this DOCUMENT.

The input provided c.803_805CCT[1]; this represents the same 3-bp deletion event (CCT×1) and likely reflects an alternate transcript/legacy cDNA coordinate; we reconciled it to NM_004700.4:c.806_808delCCT per the document. Phenotype mapping: The proband and family are described as having “autosomal dominant progressive hearing loss” with pure-tone audiometry demonstrating “moderate sensorineural hearing loss with high frequency dominance” and no syndromic features (3.1; Fig.1B). PM2_Supporting prerequisite: The variant was “found neither on the Exome Variant Server nor in the control group of 96 unrelated Japanese individuals with normal hearing” (document text near Fig.1/3 Results 3.2), supporting absence from population databases consistent with PM2_Supporting for AD per the GUIDE. Controls: Although 96 normal-hearing controls were screened and none carried the variant, this study is not a formal case-control analysis per the GUIDE; per proband-count mode, b/c/d were not populated. Independence/duplication: Only a single family is presented; one proband was sequenced. No overlap with other cohorts in the paper is indicated. Therefore, a=1, b=c=d=0; total_ps4_case_counts=1.

The abstract also contains “p.Ser260del,” which is inconsistent with the body text and Table 1; given that all detailed sections, figures, and the electropherogram (Fig. 2A) specify Ser269, we treat “p.Ser260del” as a typographical error referring to the same event (mapped by the cDNA deletion coordinates). Phenotype mapping: The proband is in “a pedigree of autosomal dominant progressive hearing loss” with moderate, high-frequency predominant sensorineural loss and no syndromic features (Sections 3.1, 5; Fig. 1A–B), which maps to the allowed phenotype: nonsyndromic genetic deafness (Autosomal dominant). Counting (proband_count mode): One unrelated family is reported; by default we count one proband per family unless independence of multiple probands is explicit. PM2_Supporting prerequisite: The authors state the variant was not found in EVS (population database) and was absent in 96 unrelated Japanese controls with normal hearing (Section 3.2: “found neither on the Exome Variant Server nor in the control group of 96 unrelated Japanese individuals with normal hearing”), supporting that PM2_Supporting is met for an AD gene per VCEP specifications. Deduplication/overlap: Only one family is presented; no evidence of overlap with other families or prior reports of this exact variant within this paper. Zygosity/inheritance: The proband is heterozygous for an autosomal dominant KCNQ4 variant; this is appropriate for counting under AD PS4. Controls: Although 96 normal-hearing controls were screened with no carriers detected, this is not a formal case-control design; per the selected PS4 proband-count mode we did not populate c/d.

Allowed phenotype includes malignant hyperthermia of anesthesia (RYR1 VCEP disease section). The study cohort comprised “73 unrelated individuals diagnosed as MH susceptible according to the North American MH caffeine–halothane contracture test” (Methods), satisfying affected status and independence by default. Variant identification: The target variant p.Asp2431Asn corresponds to p.D2431N (input alias) and c.7291G>A (input), which the paper reports as “Asp2431Asn” and “G7291A” (Table 1; Results). Mapping logic: Asp→Asn at codon 2431 equals D2431N; G7291A equals c.7291G>A. Counting: Table 1 reports Asp2431Asn in 1 unrelated MHS individual (frequency 1.4% of 73). Table 2 lists this case explicitly as US41 with positive CHCT (halothane 6.0 g; caffeine 0.7 g) and a positive family history. Therefore, a = 1 (one unrelated affected proband carrying the variant). No additional relatives with the same variant were shown for US41, so no deduplication within a family was required. Exclusions: No evidence that the US41 proband harbored multiple RYR1 VUS/LP/P variants; thus no exclusion under VCEP PS4 rules. Controls: Although 134–158 normal control chromosomes were screened with none carrying the novel substitutions, proband_count mode does not require control tallies; hence b=c=d are not populated. counting_justification:
Study-type gate: Primary case series with new genotyping of MH-susceptible individuals; therefore eligible for PS4 counting (Anesthesiology 2001; Methods).
PS4 mode: Per RYR1 VCEP v2.0.0, PS4 uses a proband-based MH case points system; we therefore operate in proband_count mode.
Variant identification and alias reconciliation: Inputs specify RYR1 p.D2431N / c.7291G>A / Chr19:38990624G>A. The document reports the same molecular event as a novel mutation in exon 45 denoted "G7291A (Asp2431Asn)" (Table 1) and lists the affected subject US41 in Table 2. The paper’s nucleotide notation omits the 'c.' prefix; mapping: c.7291G>A = G7291A; p.Asp2431Asn = p.D2431N. These are equivalent representations of the same variant. We searched for these aliases in the document and found "Asp2431Asn" and "G7291A" explicitly (Results; Table 1; Table 2).
Phenotype mapping: Allowed phenotype is malignant hyperthermia of anesthesia. All 73 individuals were diagnosed MH susceptible (MHS) by the North American CHCT, and the paper states "Patients who test positive ... are considered to be MH susceptible (MHS)" (Methods). This maps to the allowed phenotype. Table 2 provides CHCT data for US41 (0.7 g caffeine; 6.0 g halothane), confirming MHS.
Independence and deduplication: The cohort consists of "73 unrelated individuals" (Methods). For Asp2431Asn, only one unrelated proband (US41) is listed; no relatives are counted for this variant. Thus a=1 without risk of duplicate family counting. We applied the generic ACMG/AMP caution to avoid duplicate counting; here independence is explicitly stated.
Exclusion screen (multiple RYR1 variants): The VCEP excludes individuals harboring multiple RYR1 VUS/LP/P variants. The paper does not report any additional RYR1 variants in US41 beyond Asp2431Asn; therefore no exclusion applies.
Counting details: From Table 1, Asp2431Asn frequency is 1.4% among 73 unrelated MHS cases, consistent with 1/73. Table 2 explicitly lists one subject (US41) with this variant and CHCT-positive status. Accordingly, we count a=1. No control arm is present; thus b=c=d=0.
MH case points (for audit only, not applying thresholds): Per VCEP PS4 rubric, a proband with (i) personal or family history of an MH event earns 0.5 points and (ii) positive IVCT/CHCT earns +0.5 points. For US41, CHCT is positive (Table 2), and Family History is marked "Yes" while "Episode" is "No"; the VCEP permits family history of an MH event to qualify for 0.5. Therefore, we record 1.0 MH case point for this proband. Thresholds are not applied here; only point extraction is recorded.
